In Java, the code would look like:

public int maxTaskAssign(int[] tasks, int[] workers, int pills, int strength) {

    // sort tasks and workers

    Arrays.sort(tasks);

    Arrays.sort(workers);

    int low = 0, high = Math.min(tasks.length, workers.length);

    int answer = 0;

    while (low <= high) {

        int mid = (low + high) / 2;

        if (isFeasible(tasks, workers, mid, pills, strength)) {

            answer = mid;

            low = mid + 1;

        } else {

            high = mid - 1;

        }

    }

    return answer;

}

private boolean isFeasible(int[] tasks, int[] workers, int k, int pills, int strength) {

    Arrays.sort(tasks); // assuming already sorted, but in the main function, we sort once.

    Arrays.sort(workers); 

    PriorityQueue<Integer> heap = new PriorityQueue<>();

    int j = 0;

    for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) {

        // For task tasks[i], add all workers that can handle it with a pill.

        while (j < workers.length && workers[j] + strength < tasks[i]) {

            j++;

        }

        // Now add all remaining workers to the heap.

        while (j < workers.length) {

            heap.offer(workers[j]);

            j++;

        }

        // Now, check if there's a worker in the heap that can handle this task.

        if (heap.isEmpty()) {

            return false;

        }

        // If the smallest worker in the heap can handle it without pill.

        if (heap.peek() >= tasks[i]) {

            heap.poll(); // assign without pill.

        } else {

            // Need to use a pill.

            if (pills > 0) {

                heap.poll();

                pills--;

            } else {

                return false;

            }

        }

    }

    return true;

}

public int maxTaskAssign(int[] tasks, int[] workers, int pills, int strength) {

    // sort tasks and workers

    Arrays.sort(tasks);

    Arrays.sort(workers);

    int low = 0, high = Math.min(tasks.length, workers.length);

    int answer = 0;

    while (low <= high) {

        int mid = (low + high) / 2;

        if (isFeasible(tasks, workers, mid, pills, strength)) {

            answer = mid;

            low = mid + 1;

        } else {

            high = mid - 1;

        }

    }

    return answer;

}

private boolean isFeasible(int[] tasks, int[] workers, int k, int pills, int strength) {

    Arrays.sort(tasks); // assuming already sorted, but in the main function, we sort once.

    Arrays.sort(workers); 

    PriorityQueue<Integer> heap = new PriorityQueue<>();

    int j = 0;

    for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) {

        // For task tasks[i], add all workers that can handle it with a pill.

        while (j < workers.length && workers[j] + strength < tasks[i]) {

            j++;

        }

        // Now add all remaining workers to the heap.

        while (j < workers.length) {

            heap.offer(workers[j]);

            j++;

        }

        // Now, check if there's a worker in the heap that can handle this task.

        if (heap.isEmpty()) {

            return false;

        }

        // If the smallest worker in the heap can handle it without pill.

        if (heap.peek() >= tasks[i]) {

            heap.poll(); // assign without pill.

        } else {

            // Need to use a pill.

            if (pills > 0) {

                heap.poll();

                pills--;

            } else {

                return false;

            }

        }

    }

    return true;

}
